# MOC: Cancer Immunology

> **Cancer-immune system interactions, immunotherapy mechanisms, and anti-tumor immunity**
> 
> How the immune system recognizes, attacks, and sometimes fails to eliminate cancer cells — and how we can enhance anti-tumor immunity.

---

## 🎯 Entry Points (Start Here)

**Core Mechanisms:**
- [[Aspirin_TXA2_Mechanism_Metastasis_Prevention_Z-002-0875]] — Platelet-mediated immune suppression
- [[Platelet_COX1_Inhibition_T_Cell_Activation_Z-002-0876]] — Molecular pathway of aspirin action
- [[Micrometastases_Immune_Vulnerability_Window_Z-002-0878]] — Clinical window for intervention

**Paradigm Shifts:**
- [[Aspirin_Cancer_Immunomodulator_Not_Just_Anti_Platelet_Z-002-0877]] — Aspirin as immune modulator

---

## 📊 Core Content

### Metastasis & Immune Evasion
- `Aspirin_TXA2_Mechanism_Metastasis_Prevention_Z-002-0875` — TXA2-T-cell axis
- `Micrometastases_Immune_Vulnerability_Window_Z-002-0878` — Circulating tumor cells
- `Platelet_COX1_Inhibition_T_Cell_Activation_Z-002-0876` — Platelet-immune crosstalk

### Pharmacological Interventions
- `Aspirin_Cancer_Immunomodulator_Not_Just_Anti_Platelet_Z-002-0877` — Paradigm shift
- `Aspirin_Dosing_Cancer_Prevention_Low_Dose_Sufficient_Z-002-0879` — Practical application

### Clinical Evidence
- **Metastasis reduction:** HR 0.64 (36% risk reduction)
- **Cancer death reduction:** HR 0.49 (51% risk reduction)
- **Effective dose:** 75-300mg daily aspirin
